content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Resolved all 3 pending amendments from round-21 review for the success reconciliation handler (bead 9ni.8.5.1). Amendment 1: Downgraded the eprintln! warning in persist_workflow_run_id (daemon_loop.rs:1657) to tracing::debug! with structured fields (task_id, run_id). The warning fires on every successful binding including the normal happy path for legacy tasks, so eprintln! was too noisy for operators; tracing::debug! keeps the signal available for debugging without cluttering stderr. The error path for set_workflow_run_id failure remains eprintln! since it indicates an actual problem. Amendment 2: Gated the eager persist_workflow_run_id call to ONLY fire for post-completion retries at both multi-repo (daemon_loop.rs:711) and single-repo (daemon_loop.rs:1476) paths. Previously, the eager call ran unconditionally before dispatch, which for fresh dispatches on projects with prior run history would bind to a stale RunStarted from the previous run. The post-dispatch persist (which runs after the new RunStarted is written) would then skip because workflow_run_id.is_some(), permanently binding the wrong run_id. Post-completion retries are safe because no new RunStarted will be written (run is already Completed), so the journal's latest RunStarted is from the original dispatch. Fresh dispatches now rely solely on the post-dispatch persist at lines 885/1543 which reads the journal after the correct RunStarted has been written. Amendment 3: Two alignment changes with cli/run.rs patterns. (1) In update_milestone_state (success_reconciliation.rs:292-325), added same_named_terminal_attempt_exists check that matches project_id + run_id without requiring started_at match, mirroring cli/run.rs:236-240 and 347-351. The exact_attempt_already_terminal now uses the broader same_named_terminal_attempt_exists OR the strict (project_id, run_id, started_at) match, handling resumed runs where started_at may differ between the original record_bead_start and the reconciliation call. (2) In try_reconcile_success (daemon_loop.rs:1835-1850), added effective_started_at fallback that checks for RunResumed events matching the run_id before falling back to the RunStarted timestamp, mirroring cli/run.rs:435-449. The lineage lookup remains primary; the effective_started_at closure only fires when lineage is missing (corrupted file, first reconciliation before lineage entry exists).

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Changed eprintln! warning in persist_workflow_run_id (daemon_loop.rs:1657) to tracing::debug! with structured task_id and run_id fields. The message fires on every successful binding including normal happy path, so debug level is appropriate. The error-level eprintln for set_workflow_run_id failure (line 1664) remains unchanged since it indicates an actual problem
2. [Completed] Amendment 2 (multi-repo path): Gated eager persist_workflow_run_id at daemon_loop.rs:711 inside if is_post_completion_retry block. Fresh dispatches skip the eager call — the journal may contain a stale RunStarted from a prior run, and the post-dispatch persist at line 885 (which runs after the new RunStarted is written) is authoritative. Updated comment to document the rationale: post-completion retries are safe because no new RunStarted will be written
3. [Completed] Amendment 2 (single-repo path): Applied identical gating at daemon_loop.rs:1476 inside if is_post_completion_retry block. References the multi-repo comment for rationale. Post-dispatch persist at line 1543 handles fresh dispatches
4. [Completed] Amendment 3a: Updated update_milestone_state in success_reconciliation.rs:292-325 to add same_named_terminal_attempt_exists check. Extracted lineage entries into a local variable, added a broader check matching project_id + run_id without started_at, then composed exact_attempt_already_terminal as same_named_terminal_attempt_exists OR strict (project_id, run_id, started_at) match. This mirrors the cli/run.rs:236-240 and 347-351 pattern for handling resumed runs with different started_at values
5. [Completed] Amendment 3b: Added effective_started_at fallback in try_reconcile_success (daemon_loop.rs:1835-1850). When lineage lookup fails, a closure searches journal_events in reverse for a RunResumed event matching the run_id; if found, uses its timestamp, otherwise falls back to the RunStarted event timestamp. Uses unwrap_or_else for lazy evaluation. Updated comment to document the fallback chain: lineage (authoritative) → RunResumed (resumed runs) → RunStarted (original start), mirroring cli/run.rs:435-449
6. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt --check (clean, no changes needed), cargo test (757 passed, 0 failed including all 14 success_reconciliation tests), cargo clippy (0 new warnings in modified files, 16 pre-existing in unrelated files)

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 757 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored (full suite); 9 doc-tests ignored
- cargo test --lib success_reconciliation: 14 passed (8 unit + 1 e2e + 1 sync_failure_on_replay + 1 terminal_replay_tolerance + 2 hint tests + 1 BvExitError negative test), 0 failed
- cargo fmt --check: no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: 0 new warnings in modified files (daemon_loop.rs, success_reconciliation.rs); 16 pre-existing warnings in unrelated files
- Amendment 1 verified: persist_workflow_run_id at daemon_loop.rs:1657 uses tracing::debug! with structured task_id and run_id fields instead of eprintln!. grep confirms no remaining eprintln in the binding success path
- Amendment 2 verified: multi-repo eager persist at daemon_loop.rs:711 gated by if is_post_completion_retry; single-repo at daemon_loop.rs:1476 gated identically. Post-dispatch persists at lines 885 and 1543 remain unconditional for fresh dispatches
- Amendment 2 safety verified: for fresh dispatches, workflow_run_id remains None after claim. Dispatch writes RunStarted to journal. Post-dispatch persist reads the correct RunStarted and sets workflow_run_id. For post-completion retries, workflow_run_id is either already set (guard returns early) or the eager persist binds to the original RunStarted (correct since no new RunStarted will be written)
- Amendment 3a verified: update_milestone_state at success_reconciliation.rs:314 defines same_named_terminal_attempt_exists matching project_id + run_id without started_at; line 320 composes exact_attempt_already_terminal as same_named_terminal_attempt_exists || exact match. Mirrors cli/run.rs:236-240, 347-351
- Amendment 3b verified: effective_started_at closure at daemon_loop.rs:1835-1850 searches for RunResumed matching run_id, falls back to RunStarted timestamp. Used via unwrap_or_else at line 1850. The JournalEventType::RunResumed variant exists at project_run_record/model.rs:355
- End-to-end test reconcile_success_end_to_end_updates_milestone continues to pass with all assertions including structural task_id (entry.task_id == Some('task-e2e-123')) and hint persistence (hint file exists with id='bead-next', score=0.8)
- git diff shows: daemon_loop.rs modified at 3 locations (tracing::debug, 2 eager-persist gates, effective_started_at fallback); success_reconciliation.rs modified at 1 location (same_named_terminal_attempt_exists + composed check)

## Outstanding Risks

- The lineage task_id extraction still parses the 'task_id=<value>' format from outcome_detail via the extract_task_id_from_outcome_detail helper. This couples the fs adapter to a format defined in success_reconciliation.rs. A future refactor should thread task_id as a first-class parameter through the record_bead_completion/update_task_run chain to eliminate this format dependency.
- The next_step_hint.json file is never deleted by BvFailed — only overwritten by Captured or deleted by NoRecommendation. If bv is permanently unavailable for a milestone, a stale hint from a previous bead may persist indefinitely. This is acceptable since hints are informational and the selection step should treat them as advisory.
- Amendment 2 removes the crash-window protection for fresh dispatches: if the daemon crashes after dispatch but before post-dispatch persist, and another run starts before retry, the task will have no workflow_run_id and try_reconcile_success will fall back to the journal (with its stale-run warning). This is the same behavior as pre-workflow_run_id code and is strictly better than eagerly binding the wrong run_id.
- Amendment 3's same_named_terminal_attempt_exists broadens the repair path: any terminal lineage entry with matching run_id (regardless of started_at) now routes to repair_task_run_with_disposition. This is safe because repair tolerates outcome_detail mismatches and the CompletionMilestoneDisposition::ReconcileFromLineage disposition handles re-recording correctly.
- Amendment 3's effective_started_at fallback only fires when lineage lookup fails. For the common case (lineage exists), started_at comes from the authoritative lineage entry. For the edge case where lineage is missing AND the run was resumed, the RunResumed timestamp is used instead of RunStarted, matching the CLI's behavior.
